selectedSlice: Replace live selected-site and intervention-heavy wording in the site Mentor Assignments helper and add verifier coverage.
selectionReason: Current repo evidence showed a real protected-workspace leak in functions/_lib/site-mentor-assignments.ts: live route-backed Mentor Assignments responses still emitted selected-site and intervention wording even after the workspace copy had already moved to school-facing language. The verifier did not scan that helper yet, so this was the safest bounded batch because it fixed real protected copy and added a direct regression guard without changing routes, auth, or assignment behavior.
candidateShortlist: Broader Global Admin route refresh across Recent Audit, Students, Presentation, Programs, and Users & Access
userFacingImprovement: Live Mentor Assignments responses now say this school and follow-up checkpoint instead of selected-site and intervention wording, so site staff and read-only users see cleaner school-facing guidance in the existing protected workflow.
privacyAndPermissionNotes: The slice changes only user-facing helper copy and verifier coverage; no route shape, mutation path, auth gate, RBAC, tenant scope, or student visibility changed.
The same /api/site/mentor-assignments records, filters, and permissions remain in place.
No new controls, destinations, or data fields were introduced.

validation.notes: Focused helper-route and language-verifier coverage passed before docs/state closeout. The full validation ladder also passed after the closeout files were updated.

deferred: Broader Global Admin route refresh remains larger than this bounded Mentor Assignments helper slice.
Role-assignment scope-name enrichment is still useful but lower workflow value than fixing the live protected copy leak.
Admin mentor-meeting follow-up still lacks a precise current destination.
Program Teacher mentor-meeting follow-up still lacks a precise scoped destination.
Hosted section-level permission proof still needs allowed hosted runtime and audit-event writes.
Review Queue missing-submission semantics remain deferred until backend rows or exact scoped empty-state evidence exist.
Viewer email visibility policy still needs a product decision before any UI change.
Mentor reassignment and removal workflow still needs mutation and audit design.
Student hosted drill-down refresh still needs a broader hosted/product pass.
nextRecommended: Resume the bounded Global Admin route refresh only where the current workspace can prove an exact destination, and keep mentor-meeting follow-up summary-only until a precise backed path exists.
doNotRepeat: Do not let functions/_lib/site-mentor-assignments.ts reintroduce selected-site wording into protected Mentor Assignments copy.
Do not reintroduce intervention-heavy checkpoint wording where school-facing follow-up language already exists.
Do not widen Mentor Assignments permissions or add new mutation controls as part of copy-only hardening.
